summary: Verified the CRITICAL cart bug fix. Backend pytest 7/7 PASS for PUT /api/cart/items/{item_id}, DELETE /api/cart/items/{item_id}, POST /api/cart/coupon (WELCOME10), DELETE /api/cart/coupon. Frontend Playwright e2e on /cart confirms: + increases qty, - decreases qty, trash icon deletes item, cart counter and subtotal update correctly, WELCOME10 applies and shows 'applied' badge, X removes the coupon. User-reported blocker (cart update/delete) is RESOLVED. However found one related UI regression in Order Summary — see action_items.

minor.issue: Backend uses shipping=75 for orders < R399 (server.py:1391), but frontend CartPage.js uses hardcoded SHIPPING_COST=65 (line 15). Frontend total will diverge from backend total by R10. Standardise the value (env or fetch from /api/cart).

ui_bugs.issue: When WELCOME10 is applied, backend GET /api/cart returns discount=29.8 and total=343.2, but Order Summary shows NO 'Discount' line and the Total stays at R363.00 (uncorrected). Root cause: CartPage.js:82 reads `couponApplied?.discount`, but POST /api/cart/coupon response is `{coupon:{code, discount_type, discount_value}}` — there is no `discount` key. Fix: use `cart.discount` (already returned by GET /api/cart via CartContext) instead of local couponApplied state, OR map discount_value/discount_type into a numeric discount. Also detect existing coupon from `cart.coupon_code` on mount so the applied badge persists across reloads.
Shipping is computed client-side with hardcoded R65, but backend computes R75 for sub-R399 orders. Use cart.shipping from backend.

handleApplyCoupon/handleRemoveCoupon in CartPage.js call raw axios endpoints; they currently work only because AuthContext sets axios.defaults.headers.common.Authorization globally. Prefer using applyCoupon/removeCoupon from CartContext for consistency with the rest of the cart actions (those add X-Session-ID for guests).

integration_issues.issue: Coupon applied successfully on backend (discount=29.8, total=343.2) but UI Total does not reflect it (still 363). Misleads users into thinking the coupon didn't take effect.

action_items: [HIGH] Fix Order Summary discount/total. In CartPage.js, derive `discount` from `cart.discount` (provided by GET /api/cart via CartContext) and `total` from `cart.total` so the order summary reflects the backend-computed values after a coupon is applied. Also restore `couponApplied` state on initial load when `cart.coupon_code` is non-null so the 'applied' badge survives page reload.
[MEDIUM] Replace hardcoded SHIPPING_COST=65 in CartPage.js with `cart.shipping` from backend (or unify the threshold/cost between frontend and backend).
[LOW] Refactor handleApplyCoupon/handleRemoveCoupon to use applyCoupon/removeCoupon from CartContext (consistency + guest cart X-Session-ID support).
[Carry-over from iteration_7] /api/admin/orders 500 (server.py:2552 shipping float vs dict) still needs the defensive `isinstance(...,dict)` guard.
critical_code_review_comments: CartContext.updateQuantity / removeFromCart now correctly use PUT and DELETE on /cart/items/{item_id} with Authorization + X-Session-ID headers — confirmed working end-to-end.
Backend item_id parsing uses `rsplit('_', 1)` (server.py:1466, 1497) which works for IDs like `fynbos-roast_fynbos-250g-whole`. Verified.
CartPage.js maintains a parallel `couponApplied` state that diverges from CartContext.cart — single source of truth would be cleaner.
Hardcoded shipping in two places (frontend CartPage R65, backend cart.py R75) — schema drift risk.
Order Summary VAT computation is client-side from local subtotal/shipping; once discount is fixed, ensure VAT is also re-derived from backend `cart.vat` (already returned).

success_rate.frontend: 85% (qty+/-/trash/coupon-apply/coupon-remove all work; Order Summary Total does not reflect discount)

test_credentials: testuser2@capeember.co.za / Test1234! — login OK, token returned, cart endpoints authorised.
seed_data_creation: Tests add and clear cart items on testuser2 between test cases; no other persistent data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cart update/delete user-blocker is fixed and verified (backend pytest + Playwright). Remaining open: Order Summary on /cart does not show Discount line nor adjusted Total when a coupon is applied — CartPage.js needs to read discount/total from cart (CartContext) rather than from couponApplied.discount (which doesn't exist in the apply-coupon response). Shipping cost mismatch frontend(65) vs backend(75). Carry-over: admin/orders 500 bug from iteration_7 still open.
rca of the issue: ORIGINAL BUG RCA: CartContext.updateQuantity/removeFromCart were calling the wrong endpoints / missing auth headers. Fix in CartContext.js now PUTs to `/api/cart/items/{productId_variantId}` and DELETEs the same path with Authorization + X-Session-ID — confirmed by curl/pytest and Playwright. NEW UI BUG RCA (Order Summary): POST /api/cart/coupon returns `{message, coupon:{code, discount_type, discount_value}}` (no top-level `discount` numeric). CartPage.js stores this object into `couponApplied` then reads `couponApplied?.discount` which is undefined → discount=0 → Total never decreases. Backend cart object (GET /api/cart) does contain the correctly computed `discount` and `total`; the page should consume those.
